text: He is competent and understands the material. Tests are not too difficult. He doesn't grade homework quickly at all but other than that I have no complaints.

i had him for intro to discrete and didn't recognize his name when choosing classes and regretted it. he is a tough grader and blames students when the entire class average on an exam is low. only good part is he records lectures

I think he is a good professor, I really do. He is good at explaining topics, especially the videos uploaded after lectures. This class is just brutally difficult. The concepts are so complex and the exams are very challenging. I would take it with him, and the TA's are really helpful, but prepare to spend countless hours studying.

Dr. Rahaman is a great professor for cs2. His lectures are great even though i was not enrolled in his section. I would attend his lectures when i couldn't go to mine. Explains topics well and i was able to code up the examples on my own after his explanations

Dr. Rahaman did a great job explaining complex topics and concepts in an easily digestible manner. He is encouraging and caring. There are some extensive homework assignments that take a lot of time. He puts in the effort to prepare you for test material, but it is on you to truly practice material and put in the time outside of class.

This is for COP 3502H. CS1 is tough class in general and as an IT major really sucked. Regardless, Professor Rahaman was very kind, gave extra credit, and quizzes were manageable. Midterm and final were okay, but prog assignments helped somewhat. He is good for CS, IT, and other majors, but CS1 is just tough in general regardless of professor.

Rahaman is nice, but his lectures feel dry and boring to follow. He admits he's more used to teaching C than Java, which shows since it feels like a weaker version of Steinberg's material. Tests and assignments are easy except for a DP assignment. Disorganized, late test case updates, and very delayed grading. Easy class, but not much learning.

Terrifying Class. Was his first year of teaching so the final bits were rushed. CS1 is a HARD class, no matter who is teaching. He is very skilled in the subject and is learning how to convey that to others. Class was HEAVILY curved with him and was the only class that didn't have a mandatory "you need this % on final or you fail automatically".
Best professor for CS1 if you are a non CS major. Very easy course, with lots of extra credit. Most of the lecture he just read off PowerPoint slides, majority of my learning was done on my own time.

Lectures consist of him reading others' slides word for word and you can tell he doesn't read them before teaching them. A big chunk of the lab assignments had incorrect answers which students had to correct. Exams are done online, only final answers matter, not your work. Final exam average was 44%, he curved 26% back. Get ready to teach yourself.

Absolutely the best professor that teaches CS1. This was his first year teaching it and he did a great job. Gave us so many opportunities for extra credit and gave really great curves for both the midterm and final. Lectures were recorded so you can watch them on your on time. Labs did not require attendance and some quizzes were online. GOAT

Really nice professor, the material is hard but if you do the exercise pdfs and pay attention in lectures you should be fine. Attendance was not mandatory and the lectures were recorded, plenty of material was given to study and he would always stay after class to answer students' questions. Would take again, he's a kind person.

Be prepared to use tutoring because the lectures and slides are NOT enough. No required textbook, so definitely keep SARC / YouTube on hand for extra help. Typing out the homework was pretty annoying. He did give us a curve at the end, which was generous. He's a kind professor and very fair for such a rigorous course and never hesitated to help.
Overall a solid professor. He was able to explain topics well and was fair with exam material.
The course content was difficult for me but the professor was always easy to reach and a pleasure to speak with. Exams were weighted very high but he was very generous with extra credit and curves.

The course is so hard that the average for the final was a 44%. The homework is typed only, which, for a math course, sucked, so r the exams. Everything is lockdown browser except Homework and Labs. No textbook, all you have are his slides, and they suck. He threatened to have quizzes during class to force people to come; this never happened tho.

He was good at explaining the materials. We had the online recordings of the lectures (zoom) which helped me a lot. The tests were fair; if you go through the PowerPoint and do a few examples you will be fine. He was very understanding and cared about the students.
